bull: RSI 29 and stochastic 3.8 are deeply oversold after a 14.5% one-day drop, a level that has historically preceded short-term bounces.
Robotics (Optimus) optionality remains a long-dated call option on the story that today's panic-selling didn't reprice on fundamentals.
bear: Price sits 21% below its 50-day and 23% below its 200-day SMA with a strongly negative MACD — the trend is broken, not just noisy.
Today's crash was part of a broad Mag-7 rout (-$797B in one day, worst since April 2025), so there's no company-specific floor being defended.
TSLA.summary: Tesla was swept up in the worst Magnificent-7 sell-off since the April 2025 tariff shock, falling 14.5% on heavy volume with RSI/stochastics at capitulation extremes; a technical bounce is plausible but the trend and catalyst backdrop remain bearish.
TSLA.key_risk: Further Mag-7 de-rating or a weak delivery/margin update could extend the move well past the current oversold reading.

bull: Termite and commercial pest-control segments posted strong growth per the Q2 call, and the category is historically defensive.
RSI 34.5 and a 40% pullback from 52-week highs leave room for mean reversion if residential demand stabilizes.
bear: Third straight quarter of missing lofty expectations, with BofA flagging decreased residential demand visibility going forward.
Price is 15.6% below its 50-day and 28.5% below its 200-day SMA on the highest volume ratio in the deep set (5.7x) — a distribution, not accumulation, signature.
ROL.summary: Rollins fell 9.3% after a third consecutive earnings miss, with residential demand softening and heavy distribution volume; the growth story is intact in commercial/termite but the market is repricing the deceleration.
ROL.key_risk: A fourth consecutive guidance disappointment would confirm a structural residential slowdown rather than a one-off.

bull: EPS beat with raised 2026 free cash flow guidance and 8% revenue growth — the fundamentals improved even as the stock fell.
RSI 43.7 is neutral, not broken down, and the sell-off looks disproportionate to a beat-and-raise quarter.
bear: Revenue missed estimates and postpaid growth deceleration is a recurring market worry for telecom multiples.
Rejecting an expanded Starlink partnership removes a potential rural-coverage growth lever the market may have been pricing in.
TMUS.summary: T-Mobile dropped 10.8% despite an EPS beat and raised cash-flow guidance, largely on a revenue miss and broad market panic; the reaction looks overdone relative to the underlying print.
TMUS.key_risk: Postpaid subscriber growth deceleration becoming a persistent trend rather than a one-quarter blip.

bull: Q2 beat drove price-target hikes from Bernstein and RBC on strong contract revenue growth, even as the stock fell with the market.
RSI 29.7 and stochastic 1.8 are at capitulation extremes despite the fundamental beat — a rare oversold-plus-good-news combination.
bear: UBS flagged 'mixed demand' signals and some analysts think favorable deal timing flattered the headline beat.
20-day momentum divergence (-9.6) shows the stock was already rolling over before today's broader Mag-7-style drop.
NOW.summary: ServiceNow fell 3.7% (down 11.6% over 5 days) despite a genuine Q2 beat and analyst price-target hikes, landing it at oversold RSI extremes — a beat that got sold into a panicky tape.
NOW.key_risk: If UBS's 'mixed demand' read proves correct, the stock could re-rate lower even after the beat wears off as a catalyst.

bull: Commentary from Mizuho and Barchart argues EDA software wins regardless of who leads the AI race — a structurally advantaged, low-competition moat.
RSI 22.8, among the most oversold names in the set, after a 19.5% 20-day drawdown with no company-specific negative catalyst identified.
bear: Down 10.4% over 5 days as part of a broader AI/semiconductor-adjacent software de-rating, and momentum remains negative (MACD -22.3).
Price 19% below its 50-day SMA shows the drawdown is trend-confirmed, not just a single bad day.
SNPS.summary: Synopsys is deeply oversold (RSI 22.8) after a 19.5% one-month slide despite bullish sell-side commentary that EDA benefits structurally from the AI buildout regardless of which chip vendor wins.
SNPS.key_risk: A slowdown in semiconductor design-start spending would undercut the 'wins either way' thesis directly.

bull: Preliminary results showed a strong gross-margin forecast and a $60B order backlog, driving a 20%+ rally on the print.
Momentum is clearly turning: +26.4% over 5 days with RSI 63.4, a large positive momentum divergence versus the prior 20-day slide.
bear: The $60B backlog could require fresh capital to fund, raising dilution risk, and revenue growth itself is reportedly stalling.
Stock is still 50% below its 52-week high, reflecting lingering credibility/accounting overhang from prior periods.
SMCI.summary: Supermicro jumped over 20% on a strong margin forecast and record backlog, but analysts caution the $60B backlog may require a capital raise even as headline growth stalls — a real positive catalyst with a dilution shadow.
SMCI.key_risk: An equity or convertible raise to fund the backlog would dilute holders and could cap the rally.

bull: Q2 beat with record wireless additions and Peacock's first profitable quarter, plus a clear NBCUniversal/Sky separation catalyst underway.
RSI 32.6 and a 32% pullback from 52-week highs leave room for a re-rating if the spin-off progresses as planned.
bear: Broadband and cable subscriber losses persist structurally, and reported revenue actually declined year-over-year despite the 'beat'.
Falling 6.8% on an EPS beat suggests the market is more focused on the secular broadband decline than the wireless/Peacock wins.
CMCSA.summary: Comcast beat Q2 estimates on record wireless growth and a first profitable Peacock quarter, but persistent broadband losses and a revenue decline drove a 6.8% drop — a mixed, transition-year print ahead of the NBCU/Sky separation.
CMCSA.key_risk: Broadband subscriber losses accelerating faster than wireless/streaming can offset before the separation completes.

bull: Stock is already down 72% from its 52-week high with RSI 37.4, a very large cushion of bad news already priced in ahead of the July 28 report.
Homes.com and Apartments.com residential build-out plus commercial expansion remain the intact long-term growth levers highlighted heading into earnings.
bear: Q2 earnings haven't been reported yet (due July 28), so today's move is sentiment/market-driven rather than fundamental, adding event-risk uncertainty.
Price remains 46% below its 200-day SMA — a severe, sustained downtrend that a single earnings beat is unlikely to fully reverse.
CSGP.summary: CoStar drifted down 2% ahead of its July 28 earnings report, already 72% off its 52-week high; the setup is a coin-flip pending the print rather than a clear read either way.
CSGP.key_risk: A miss on residential (Homes.com) traction at the upcoming report could extend the multi-quarter downtrend further.

bull: Sell-side coverage (Zacks) continues to favor ISRG's expanding robotic-surgery ecosystem over peers like Abbott on long-term growth grounds.
RSI 22.3 is among the most oversold readings in the set with no ISRG-specific negative headline — this reads as a name caught in the broad tape, not a broken thesis.
bear: Down 17.4% over 5 days and 17.4% over 20 days, a severe drawdown that shows real distribution, not just noise.
45% off 52-week highs and 31% below its 200-day SMA — a trend break that a bull thesis has to argue is already fully priced.
ISRG.summary: Intuitive Surgical is down sharply (-17% over both 5 and 20 days, RSI 22.3) purely alongside the broader market rout, with analyst commentary still constructive on its robotic-surgery moat versus MedTech peers.
ISRG.key_risk: Any signs of procedure-volume deceleration at hospitals would remove the moat argument that's currently supporting the bull case.

bull: RSI 29.3 and 81.6% off 52-week highs leaves an extremely compressed valuation base; any earnings beat could produce an outsized snap-back.
Ad-tech spend recovery narratives (seen elsewhere in the sector) could still play out for TTD if the print surprises positively.
bear: Jefferies explicitly called the setup into earnings 'uncompelling ... in either direction,' flagging structural ad-tech competitive changes.
Momentum divergence is negative (-7.05) and the stock is 44.6% below its 200-day SMA, one of the weakest trend readings in the deep set.
TTD.summary: Trade Desk is deeply oversold and down 81.6% from its 52-week high heading into an earnings print that Jefferies itself calls an unclear setup, making this a binary, event-driven name rather than a clean directional call.
TTD.key_risk: Confirmation of the structural ad-tech competitive pressure Jefferies flagged would validate the downtrend rather than reverse it.

bull: Trading at an 11x P/E while repurchasing roughly 8% of shares annually — a cheap valuation with real capital return underneath it.
20-day momentum is strongly positive (+31.8%) and price sits 23% above its 50-day SMA, confirming a genuine uptrend into the July 28 print.
bear: RSI 80.9 and stochastic 88 are firmly overbought — this is a stretched trade, not a fresh entry point.
A previously rejected buyout offer remains an overhang, and momentum divergence (-33.1) shows the 5-day trend has already stalled versus the 20-day run.
PYPL.summary: PayPal's cheap valuation and aggressive buyback have driven a strong 20-day rally into next week's earnings, but RSI near 81 signals the move is overbought and due for consolidation before the print, not further immediate upside.
PYPL.key_risk: An overbought unwind could compound with any earnings disappointment on July 28 for a sharper-than-usual pullback.

bull: Momentum has been strong (+21.9% over 20 days) and the stock sits near its 52-week high, reflecting real investor demand.
FDA Breakthrough Device news flow in its broader diagnostics ecosystem (e.g., SkylineDx) points to continued innovation tailwinds in the space.
bear: Analysts project a single-digit earnings decline at the upcoming quarterly report, which is a mismatch versus the stock's recent momentum.
RSI 66.7 with price already up against its Bollinger upper band leaves little room for further multiple expansion before the print.
TECH.summary: Bio-Techne has rallied nearly 22% over the past month into a report where analysts actually expect an earnings decline — a scout candidate whose momentum has run ahead of the fundamental setup.
TECH.key_risk: A confirmed earnings decline at the next report would sharply expose the gap between recent price momentum and underlying results.

bull: Property & casualty insurance is a classic defensive sector, and TRV is sitting at a 52-week high with strong momentum (+17.3% over 20 days) even as growth stocks sold off today.
Rising above both 50-day and 200-day SMAs confirms a durable uptrend, not just a bounce.
bear: RSI 71.7 and stochastic 95.8 are firmly overbought, and Zacks itself is questioning whether the run can continue from a 52-week high.
Momentum divergence is negative (-5.93), hinting the very strong 20-day run has started to decelerate over the last 5 days.
TRV.summary: Travelers is a defensive standout hitting new 52-week highs on strong momentum while growth names crashed, but RSI near 72 signals the move is stretched and due for at least a pause.
TRV.key_risk: A rotation back out of defensives once the growth-stock panic subsides could quickly unwind the overbought reading.

bull: Raised 2026 guidance on stronger execution, faster innovation and record margins per the Q2 call, with UBS citing a 'multi-year growth story taking shape.'
Benefiting from read-through to the Microsoft AI infrastructure buildout, and on pace for its best week since February.
bear: RSI 65 with price near the top of its Bollinger band (92.9 position) means much of the good news may already be reflected in the price.
Broader industrial names are exposed to the same macro/tariff uncertainty rattling markets today, capping near-term upside.
MMM.summary: 3M is having its best week since February after raising 2026 guidance on stronger execution and margins, with sell-side commentary turning constructive on a multi-year growth story — a rare held position with clean positive momentum.
MMM.key_risk: Guidance already being raised once this quarter means the bar for the next update is now higher.

bull: 5-day momentum has turned sharply positive (+11.2%) against a negative 20-day trend, a divergence (+35.5) suggesting the recent slide is stabilizing.
Price remains 58% above its 200-day SMA, and AI-datacenter chip demand remains the core long-term narrative despite near-term noise.
bear: Motley Fool highlights the growing revenue gap versus Nvidia (34x) as evidence Marvell is structurally the smaller player in the AI buildout, not catching up.
Still 12.2% below its 50-day SMA and RSI at 35.5 shows the stock hasn't fully confirmed a trend reversal yet.
MRVL.summary: Marvell's 5-day bounce (+11.2%) is diverging positively from its weak 20-day trend, but commentary highlighting its widening revenue gap versus Nvidia keeps the longer-term competitive picture uncertain for this held position.
MRVL.key_risk: Continued market share erosion to Nvidia in AI networking/custom silicon would undercut the recovery thesis.

bull: Q2 beat driven by record revenue and index/FinTech growth, with the earnings call highlighting strategic AI initiatives and record Market Services results.
Price remains above both 50-day and 200-day SMAs, confirming an intact uptrend for this held position.
bear: Financial stocks broadly softened this afternoon per sector-wide coverage, meaning some of today's dip is sector rotation rather than NDAQ-specific.
RSI 63.1 leaves only modest room before the name becomes technically overbought.
NDAQ.summary: Nasdaq Inc. posted a genuine Q2 beat with record revenue and AI-initiative progress, and remains in an intact uptrend above both key moving averages despite a soft day for financials broadly.
NDAQ.key_risk: A broader rotation out of financials, if it persists, could weigh on NDAQ even without company-specific bad news.

bull: New Section 301 tariffs on Brazilian steel imports improve domestic pricing power for US producers like Nucor, per Motley Fool coverage.
Price is 29% above its 200-day SMA with strong, confirmed momentum (+2.3% over 5 days) for this held position.
bear: RSI 72.8 and stochastic 89.2 are firmly overbought, leaving the stock stretched after its run.
Steel pricing and volumes remain inherently cyclical, and any rollback or exemption to the tariff could remove the current tailwind quickly.
NUE.summary: Nucor is benefiting from fresh tariff protection on Brazilian steel imports and is up against its 52-week high with confirmed momentum, though RSI near 73 shows the move is due for a pause.
NUE.key_risk: Tariff policy is politically reversible and steel demand is cyclical, so the current pricing tailwind is not structurally guaranteed.

bull: Pentagon awarded Oracle a nearly $7B, up-to-10-year software consolidation deal, and Simply Wall St. raised its fair-value estimate on AI growth prospects.
RSI 33.3 shows the stock is oversold after a 23.5% 20-day drawdown, well below where the fresh government win would suggest it should trade.
bear: Broader commentary about Big Tech's $1.65T in off-balance-sheet AI-related debt is squarely relevant to Oracle's aggressive AI-datacenter buildout spending.
Price is 30% below its 50-day and 36% below its 200-day SMA — a severe, sustained downtrend that one government contract doesn't fully offset.
ORCL.summary: Oracle landed a major $7B Pentagon deal and saw its analyst fair value raised, but sits deeply oversold within a 23%-over-20-days downtrend as investors weigh AI-capex debt concerns against the growth story for this held position.
ORCL.key_risk: If off-balance-sheet AI infrastructure debt/lease obligations become a market-wide re-rating catalyst, ORCL is one of the most exposed names given its aggressive buildout.

bull: RSI 17.6 is the most extreme oversold reading in the entire deep set, and Morgan Stanley remains constructive on aerospace/defense heading into Q2 earnings.
TransDigm's aftermarket-parts monopoly moat is a structural advantage unaffected by the broader market's daily sentiment swings.
bear: Co-COO Joel Reiss recently exercised options and cut his stake by 46% (~$3.2M in gains), an insider-selling signal worth weighing even if routine.
Q3 earnings haven't been reported yet, with analysts expecting only single-digit growth, adding event risk on top of the technical extreme.
TDG.summary: TransDigm is at the most oversold technical extreme in the deep set (RSI 17.6) for this held position, with a resilient aerospace-aftermarket moat and a bullish sector backdrop from Morgan Stanley, tempered by recent insider stock sales and an unreported Q3 print.
TDG.key_risk: Recent insider selling combined with an unreported earnings date ahead means the oversold technical read could still be validated by fundamentals rather than reversed.
